other_observers: None; I am the finder; I reported the birds on Cobirds, but i don't know if anyone else subsequently looked for them.
specific_location: Wooded brushy lot north of the national forest maintenance yard. This yard is a half mile or so north of Crow Valley Campground on Weld Co. Road 77.
description: I will not spend too much time on the identification of these birds, since it is quite obvious and has become very familiar to Colorado birders over this winter of a major redpoll irruption.  Small streaky finches with distinctive red caps ("polls") at the forecrown. There is no other species with which this bird can be confused except for the well-known debate about common versus hoary. This flock did not contain any birds that i would consider a good candidate for hoary.Behaviors: Flying back and forth between two dead trees, perching in the open. Occasional trips to the ground to a patch of snow to eat snow (drink).Call: Chittering characteristic of flocks of these birdsPlumage: Basic
